raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding continued unauthorized charges on my Citi replacement credit card, despite having previously reported the fraud and received a replacement. 

In late XXXX, my replacement credit card was stolen before it was ever delivered to me. During that time, multiple unauthorized transactions occurred, totaling {$700.00}, which I reported and disputed with Citi. Subsequently, Citi issued another replacement card. 

Despite this, I later discovered recurring XXXX membership charges linked to another individual 's account that have continued for XXXX months using my replacement card ( {$9.00} each on XX/XX/year>, XX/XX/year>, XX/XX/year>, and XX/XX/year> ). 

This is particularly concerning since the charges continued even after the card replacement following confirmed fraud incidents. Based on this, it appears possible that the payment credentials from the compromised replacement card may have been automatically carried over, reissued, or updated onto the new card through a digital wallet, tokenized payment system, or card-on-file updater process. I can not confirm the exact mechanism, but the continued charges after replacement suggest a systemic failure in Citi 's safeguards designed to protect and reissue replacement cards securely.
